grades.expectation: Response identifies 68 years as a critical lease length issue
grades.reason: Clearly states 68 years is 'short enough to matter materially' and 'clearly in short-lease territory'

grades.expectation: Response mentions specific lender names and their minimum requirements (not just 'most lenders want 70+ years')
grades.reason: No specific lender names mentioned, only generic references to 'many mainstream lenders' and 'some lenders'

grades.expectation: Response mentions marriage value applies below 80 years
grades.reason: Explicitly states 'marriage value being payable on a statutory lease extension' below 80 years

grades.expectation: Response discusses lease extension options (statutory Section 42, informal, assign benefit)
grades.reason: Discusses statutory extension under 1993 Act and mentions seller can 'start the process and assign the benefit' to buyer

grades.expectation: Response quantifies the price impact (discount percentage or range)
grades.reason: Mentions buyers may expect price discount but provides no specific percentages or ranges

grades.expectation: Response mentions reduced buyer pool due to mortgage restrictions
grades.reason: States 'this can affect your buyer pool' and discusses lender restrictions
